checks.finding: Every REQ-001..009 maps to concrete, fixture-driven or grep/SHA-256/argv-composition-based acceptance criteria (AC-001..AC-054). The round-2 Critical (AC-027's test.yml registration claim, previously contradicted by the newly-protected file) is resolved: AC-027 is a three-part check (staged-candidate+MANIFEST.sha256 existence, live-file SHA-256-unchanged proof, post-human-cp self-registration grep), and acceptance-tests.md's Notes confirm parts (a)/(c) are fixture-driven (deterministic, no real human-in-the-loop required in CI) while part (b) is a real-file SHA-256 comparison mirroring TEST-004's established pattern. AC-053/TEST-053 and AC-054/TEST-054 give the two other round-2 Major gaps (requested-effort/welded carve-out, B1 malformed-registry rejection) executable, fixture-driven surfaces. Residual Minor: T-007's Main Workflow entry does not explicitly restate the human-copy staging step for its own new tests/effort-policy-flip suite if test.yml remains protected at that later point; non-blocking since the pattern is already established elsewhere in-spec and T-007 is a separate release.

REQ-001 through REQ-009 each have explicit AC coverage confirmed by direct read of the Acceptance Criteria section and the acceptance-tests.md mapping table (AC-001..AC-054, 1:1 with TEST-001..TEST-054, verified gap-free per the precheck edit_summary and my own sequential read of the table).

Acceptance criteria specify externally observable outcomes: schema field presence/values (AC-001..003), SHA-256 byte-identity (AC-004, AC-006, AC-007, AC-027(b)), exit-code/diagnostic-class behavior (AC-054, AC-052), JSON key attribution (AC-012), and argv-composition assertions (AC-035..040). No AC in this round relies on unobservable intent.

Non-goals section (requirements.md) explicitly excludes model-generation refresh, codex CLI argument-handling rewrite, risk-adaptive-layer work, defaulting matrix policy in Phase 1, an automated CI release-ordering gate, direct edits to any protected gate file (now including test.yml per the round-3 remedy), edits to four named protected test suites, and tasks.md/traceability.md. Phase 1 vs Phase 2 boundary is reiterated consistently across Overview, Field Definitions, and Main Workflows.

Compatibility (v1 byte-frozen, AC-004/AC-006), migration (v1/v2 coexistence, OQ-001 resolved), security (Security Boundaries B1..B4, each now with an executable verification AC), and approval boundaries (Roles and Permissions: human maintainer runs cp for all protected-file staging including test.yml as of the round-2/3 remedy, verifies SHA-256 before Done, executes T-007's prerequisite gate) are all explicit and internally consistent across every section I cross-checked (Assumptions, Roles and Permissions, Main Workflows, Risks, Edge Cases).

Risks section enumerates Critical/High/High/Medium/Medium/Low items, each tied to a specific mitigation and AC (Critical->AC-019 self-check; golden-baseline staleness High->AC-007 negative self-check; T-007-before-A3 High->AC-045/AC-046; shared-registration Medium->human-copy staging now covering test.yml; toml/selector drift Medium->AC-038; new-suite-skip Low->self-registration grep). No high-risk claim lacks a planned validation surface.
